Night-time travel safety has become a critical concern in urban environments where increasing crime rates and unforeseen incidents pose risks to individuals commuting after dark. This situation results in heightened anxiety, lower confidence in

Night-time safety has become a major concern in today’s rapidly urbanizing world, as rising crime rates and limited emergency access during late hours increase vulnerability—especially
theft, harassment, and delayed assistance often causes anxiety, reducing public confidence and

depend on manual SOS activation and static tracking, offering limited predictive capability and minimal real-time situational awareness. These systems may also fail in low- connectivity areas and lack continuous monitoring, reducing their effectiveness during emergencies.To overcome these challenges, the proposed Night Safe system introduces an AI-powered framework that integrates predictive analytics, geospatial intelligence, and real-time alert mechanisms to enhance personal safety. It uses machine

monitor user movement during travel. Automated emergency features notify guardians or nearby authorities in critical situations. The platform also includes real-time cab booking and verification to ensure ride authenticity and prevent unsafe travel.Additionally, users can report theft or suspicious incidents through live map feedback, dynamically updating risk zones for better awareness. Voice recognition and real-time image capture enable hands-free emergency activation and evidence collection. Supported by a robust backend, map APIs, and hybrid internet–SMS communication, Night Safe ensures uninterrupted protection even in low-network regions.
integration with smart city systems and law enforcement, the platform delivers a scalable and comprehensive solution for safer night travel.
Existing literature highlights that personal safety during night-time travel has become a critical challenge in modern cities, with increasing crime rates and
delayed emergency responses posing serious risks to individuals. Studies show that unsafe routes, limited surveillance, and unpredictable threats contribute

Researchers emphasize that predictive analysis using artificial intelligence and geospatial data can help identify high- risk zones and alert users in advance. Poor street lighting, unmonitored areas, and lack of real-time information further intensify vulnerability during late-hour commutes. Consequently, there is a growing need for scalable, technology-driven solutions that can combine machine learning, GPS tracking, and alert automation to enhance safety and awareness in urban environments.
Digital AI-based safety and alert systems, including mobile applications and geospatial monitoring platforms, have been widely explored as effective tools to enhance

tracking and data analytics to predict risk levels in specific areas. However, most existing safety applications are limited to manual SOS triggers or depend solely on internet connectivity, which reduces reliability during emergencies.

Literature indicates that scalable, hybrid systems combining artificial intelligence, machine learning, and offline communication channels are essential to ensure continuous protection and rapid alert delivery.
Individuals often face safety risks during night-time travel due to limited situational
especially in areas with poor lighting or low public presence. Existing safety systems largely depend
sudden or high-stress situations. The absence of predictive intelligence and real-time monitoring

night-shift workers are particularly at risk due to irregular travel patterns and lack of access to reliable safety platforms. Studies identify these issues as major barriers to ensuring consistent personal security. Reliable prediction models and automated alert mechanisms
Automated alert and notification systems have proven effective in improving personal safety and emergency responsiveness, particularly for individuals traveling during night hours or
to guardians or nearby authorities, these systems ensure that users receive immediate assistance
escalation during connectivity loss. This minimizes delays in communication and ensures that safety

Such automation also reduces dependency on user action, making the system reliable in panic or unconscious situations. Studies indicate that automated alert systems significantly enhance user confidence, reduce response time, and help prevent critical incidents. They are particularly beneficial for vulnerable populations, including women, students, and night-shift workers, who may otherwise face delayed help. Overall, these systems contribute to safer,
smarter, and more connected urban mobility.
for women, students, and night-shift workers. Fear of theft, harassment, and delayed help reduces confidence and mobility at night. Existing safety apps mainly rely on manual
minimal real-time awareness, and weak performance in low-connectivity areas.

The proposed Night Safe system addresses these gaps through an AI-powered framework that combines predictive analytics, geospatial intelligence, and real-time alerts to improve personal safety. It uses machine learning to detect crime-prone zones,
alert guardians or authorities. The platform also supports cab verification, live incident reporting with dynamic risk updates, voice-activated emergency triggers, and real- time
image capture for evidence. With a robust
internet– SMS communication, live tracking, safety scores, and smart city integration, Night Safe provides a scalable and comprehensive solution for safer night travel.
In addition to these capabilities, the Night Safe system emphasizes reliability and
proactive warnings when users approach high-risk areas.

Many available safety tools operate solely through online communication. In areas with weak or no network coverage, these applications become ineffective, preventing users from sending alerts or receiving live location
Current systems often require users to manually press an SOS button during emergencies.
time or ability to perform this action, delaying help and reducing response

Existing solutions display crime-prone areas based only on historical data. They lack dynamic geofencing and real-time monitoring, which are essential for detecting when users enter unsafe zones and automatically triggering

Many safety applications fail to provide automated multi-channel communication (SMS and Internet alerts) to guardians. This limits their reliability in ensuring that alerts are received promptly, especially during connectivity failures.
The majority of current systems cannot adapt to changing city conditions or large- scale deployments. They lack a scalable architecture to process continuous data
from multiple users, reducing their effectiveness in rapidly evolving urban environments. Current systems struggle to adjust to rapidly changing
delayed responses and reduced reliability in ensuring consistent night-time safety.
The NightSafe system was designed as an integrated personal safety platform
observe user movement and identify potential risks based on dynamic assessment of surrounding
identify nearby rides and confirm journey details before starting a trip, ensuring authenticity and
alert generation, incident recording, and communication services, while the user interface provides live visualization of routes, safety zones, and risk updates. When a user’s
location indicates a potential threat or an emergency is triggered through manual or voice activation, the system automatically gathers contextual information and sends alert
alert transmission, confirming its effectiveness in real-world scenarios. The system was designed
through evolving safety datasets and community feedback to improve situational awareness over
safety solution that integrates continuous monitoring, automated alerts, and multi-channel communication

In addition, the system’s modular architecture supports future enhancements such as advanced behavior prediction, intelligent safety scoring, and deeper integration with emergency response networks for faster coordination. These advancements will further expand NightSafe’s capability to provide real-time, context-aware protection and strengthen its role in ensuring nighttime safety across smart urban ecosystems.
The system uses live analytics, geospatial monitoring, and dynamic risk assessment to identify potential threats instantly, enabling proactive safety measures instead of reactive alerts.
members, and nearby police stations through both Internet and SMS channels, ensuring faster response during emergencies.
The system operates efficiently in both online and offline modes using SMS- based
By combining live user reports with safety data, the system dynamically updates the city’s risk zones and displays theft or incident markers for improved situational awareness..
The application continuously tracks the user’s location using GPS and geofencing technology and instantly triggers warings when unsafe zones or suspicious patterns are detected.
real-time safety maps, manage emergency contacts, and receive live notifications through a simple, intuitive dashboard and allowing users to capture the suspicious images.
can be integrated with smart city infrastructures or law enforcement databases for broader urban safety management.
To enhance personal safety during night-time travel through real-time monitoring, intelligent threat detection, and automated emergency communication using an AI- powered safety framework.

The system integrates live GPS tracking, real-time cab verification, voice-activated SOS, image capture, and dynamic theft feedback mapping. It continuously monitors user movement, detects potential threats, and sends alerts to guardians and nearby police
stations through SMS and internet channels.

Evaluate the System performance is evaluated using metrics such as alert response time, tracking accuracy, verification success rate, and reliability of emergency communication under varying network conditions.
Alert accuracy may occasionally be affected in areas with weak GPS or low network connectivity, which can be optimized through improved signal handling techniques.
Limited availability of real-time official crime data may impact prediction precision, but integration with authorized public safety databases can strengthen accuracy.
techniques can improve clarity and reliability.
Dependence on external APIs may affect system performance if services become unavailable
and normalize coordinates for clustering. Integrate user GPS data with timestamped movement logs to map location behavior patterns.
to identify and label crime-prone areas.
Apply the trained model to live user data and dynamically classify current zones as safe or unsafe. Generate risk-level indicators based on cluster proximity and alert
moves, ensuring that safety alerts and recommendations reflect the most recent location and environmental conditions.
trigger SOS alerts to registered guardians through internet and SMS channels
The alert mechanism also shares the user’s live location and timestamp to enable faster response and improved situational awareness for emergency contacts.
Visual Studio Code is used as the primary development environment for implementing

real-time safety application. The platform supports both frontend and backend development, enabling seamless integration of location tracking, risk analysis, and alert mechanisms within a single workspace.
Provides real-time debugging, testing, and extension support, enabling developers to quickly detect and resolve logic or integration issues during system development.
Supports frontend development using React along with backend services for real-time processing, API integration, and communication modules for alerts and notifications.
Enables visualization and testing of real-time geospatial data, route tracking, and risk zone mapping within the development environment.
collaborative development, and maintenance of system stability.
Provides compatibility with essential libraries for machine learning, data analysis, and map rendering, enabling implementation of hotspot prediction and dynamic visualization.
Optimized performance ensures smooth execution during development, testing, and debugging even on standard hardware configurations.
from available datasets and cleaned to ensure consistency and accuracy for
clustering model and evaluate prediction performance.
high- risk zones and optimize detection accuracy
event and prepares an alert response.
Emergency notifications along with live location details are sent to registered
Newly collected data is stored and used to periodically update risk prediction models, improving system accuracy over time.
and community reports are incorporated to keep risk mapping current and reliable.
was successfully implemented and executed as a real- time personal safety platform that continuously monitored user movement and evaluated

unsafe situations. The travel verification functionality allowed users to confirm journey details before starting trips, improving reliability and reducing uncertainty. The system processed location updates, generated alerts, maintained incident logs, and displayed live route visualization along with safety indicators. When a simulated threat condition was introduced or an emergency trigger was activated, the system immediately generated alerts and shared live location details with registered emergency contacts, demonstrating reliable response behavior.
Execution results showed accurate detection of risk zones, consistent monitoring performance, and rapid alert delivery with minimal delay, validating the operational

under different test scenarios. Overall, the execution phase confirmed that NightSafe effectively delivers proactive monitoring, automated emergency response, and real- time situational awareness to enhance personal safety.

In addition, the execution results highlighted the flexibility of the modular architecture, enabling easy integration of additional safety features and continuous improvement through updated data inputs. The system demonstrated readiness for further enhancements such as predictive risk modeling, adaptive safety scoring, and expanded coordination with emergency services.

effectively addresses the growing concern of personal safety during night-time travel by integrating continuous monitoring, intelligent risk prediction, and automated communication within a unified safety framework. Conventional safety solutions largely depend on manual SOS activation or

In contrast, NightSafe incorporates real-time location tracking, machine learning–based crime risk analysis, geofencing, and automated alert mechanisms to proactively identify unsafe conditions and respond dynamically. By continuously comparing
risk detection and minimizes response time during emergencies. Through its automated alert
registered family members and guardians, enabling reliable assistance even when the user is unable to interact manually.

and incident logging while providing users with clear safety insights and real-time situational awareness. Its adaptive design allows incorporation of updated crime datasets and user feedback to refine risk assessment over time, making it a scalable and future-ready solution for smart urban environments. By combining predictive analytics, automated alerts, and continuous monitoring, NightSafe represents a significant advancement in proactive personal safety systems. It not only enhances individual
by improving responsiveness and coordination among users and emergency contacts.
The proposed NightSafe system has demonstrated strong potential in enhancing

prediction, and automated emergency response during night-time travel. However, several opportunities exist for future research and development to further improve its accuracy, scalability, and real-world applicability. One important direction is the integration of more advanced predictive analytics to analyze behavioral patterns, travel history, and contextual environmental factors for earlier and more precise risk detection.

and provide personalized safety recommendations. Another potential enhancement involves incorporating adaptive learning mechanisms that allow the system to refine risk evaluation models based on real-world usage patterns and evolving safety

improve journey authentication and trust. Integration with wearable safety devices, environmental sensors, and public monitoring infrastructures can significantly enhance situational awareness and automated threat detection. Additionally, incorporating multilingual interaction support and adaptive notification strategies would improve accessibility for diverse user groups.
Exploration of privacy-preserving data handling techniques and secure information
protection. Continuous optimization to reduce alert latency, improve scalability across multiple regions,
With these advancements, NightSafe can evolve into a more comprehensive and

across diverse urban environments. Future integration with emergency response coordination systems and large-scale analytics platforms can enable faster decision- making and improved collaboration with safety authorities.

the world for the better. The project is based on one of the 17 goals.
